Candidate datasets for validating the VGGT-MRI model

Self-contained report · two discovery sweeps + on-node download/recon verification · generated by _html/build_datasets_report.py from tools/probe_candidate_datasets.py · 2026-06-16

TL;DR. The model's bet is gated→real-time-free-breathing transfer, which is unmeasured — we need real short-axis (SAX) cine in that regime, ideally with a clean reference to score against. After two sweeps + hands-on verification, the landscape is:
  • Two real downloadable real-time free-breathing SAX-stack k-space corpora: OCMR (Cartesian, 1.5/3T, public S3) and Göttingen radial real-time bSSFP (Blumenthal/Uecker; 40 volunteers × 22–27 SAX slices, free-breathing/ungated, BART .cfl, CC-BY). These are the headline target-domain inputs. Both verified downloadable here.
  • Open 5D motion-resolved references (cardiac×respiratory) exist but are small / whole-heart, not SAX: free-running cardiopulmonary demo (N=1, ships a 5D recon — rendered below), FISS (5 vols), and the free-running fat-fraction records. Useful as Tier-B scoring targets if resliced to SAX.
  • The Tier-A grail does not exist publicly: no dataset offers same-subject paired breath-hold-gated + real-time SAX cine. All paired comparisons in the literature use private clinical data.
  • Confirmed dead-ends: fastMRI has no cardiac data; TCIA has no k-space; mridata.org has no confirmed cardiac cine; the LGE challenges are scar/atrial, not cine; no public fetal cardiac cine.
Bottom line: use OCMR + Göttingen radial as the real-time free-breathing SAX inputs (Tier-C now, Tier-B by reconstructing references from their k-space); there is no shortcut to a clean Tier-A answer key.

1 · What the model needs, and how a dataset gets used

The model consumes individual 2D short-axis slices tagged (z,t), sampled sparsely, and outputs a 3D volume at a queried cardiac phase. A usable dataset must supply a SAX multi-slice cine in the regime we want to test. The binding constraint is the scoring reference — there is no clean 3D volume inside free-breathing real-time data; it must be supplied separately or reconstructed:

TierReferenceProvesNeeds
Asame-subject paired breath-hold gated SAX stacktrue through-plane 3D fidelitypaired acquisition (no public set exists)
B5D motion-resolved recon (XD-GRASP / self-gated) from k-space3D fidelity vs the slow gold-standard pipelineraw k-space
Cnone — held-out input framesre-projection self-consistency + qualitativeany RT/FB SAX cine

Hard requirements: human cardiac SAX multi-slice stack · cine (cardiac phases) · cardiac timing recoverable · heart centered. Multiplier: raw k-space (builds the Tier-B reference + simulates artifacts).

4 · Per-dataset detail & what we'd do with each

OCMR (ocmr.info) Tier BTier C

AccessDirect public AWS S3 — ocmr.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/data/<file>.h5 + attributes CSV. No login.
FormatISMRMRD .h5 multi-coil Cartesian k-space (reader: _archive/ocmr/read_ocmr.py)
Content265 scans: gated (fs_*) + real-time undersampled (pse/us_*); SAX+LAX; 1.5T & 3T. Verified 16 real-time free-breathing SAX stacks (dur=lng, sli=stk), 10–17 slices.
LicenseCC-BY-NC-4.0
Verified✓ downloaded fs_0005 (220 MB), fs_0056 (2063 MB), us_0084 (808 MB); reconstructed + rendered.

What we can do with it: Primary Cartesian target set. Run on us_* real-time free-breathing SAX stacks → Tier-C now; reconstruct a per-scan CS/motion-resolved reference from the k-space → Tier-B. Gated fs_* stacks build/validate the recon pipeline.

Caveats: Real-time us_* are R≈7–9 → need CS/PI recon. Gated & real-time scans are different sessions → no per-subject paired (Tier-A) reference. Use the multi-slice subset. Non-commercial.

Göttingen radial real-time bSSFP (Blumenthal/Uecker, NLINV-Net) Tier BTier C

AccessPublic Zenodo, 5 parts — zenodo.org/records/10492333 (+ parts 2–5). No login.
FormatBART .cfl/.hdr radial raw k-space, per volume (volNNNN_vis1.cfl, ~3 GB each)
Content40 volunteers, 22–27 short-axis slices each, free-breathing / ungated, 5-turn interleaved radial bSSFP, ~33 ms/frame, 127 frames. vol0001 BART dims = [320, 13, 1, 34, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 1, 127, 1, 1, 24, 1, 1] (READ×spokes×coil(34)×…×frames(127)×slices(24)).
LicenseCC-BY-4.0 · ~166 GB total
Verified✓ accessibility + dims confirmed (.hdr fetched; vol0001.cfl = 3449 MB via HEAD). Full volume not pulled — radial recon needs NUFFT.

What we can do with it: The strongest NEW free-breathing SAX-stack source — more slices/subject than OCMR, fully open. Tier-C self-consistency on the real-time radial cine; Tier-B by reconstructing a reference (NLINV/NUFFT) from the k-space. Complements OCMR with a radial-trajectory domain.

Caveats: Radial (golden-angle) → recon needs NUFFT/gridding (env has sigpy); diastolic self-gating index files included. No shipped clean reference. Large (~166 GB).

Zenodo 10639392 — cine + real-time free-breathing at rest/stress 2D only

AccessPublic Zenodo — zenodo.org/records/10639392 (4 tarballs; images.tgz 7.6 GB)
FormatBART .cfl/.hdr reconstructed coil images (+ contours, end-expiration indices, nnU-Net inference)
Content15 volunteers, 3T. Per subject: breath-hold cine (25 phases) + real-time free-breathing rt/rt_stress/rt_maxstress (120–150 frames). Single SAX slice per subject.
Verified✓ stream-extracted vol14, coil-combined + rendered.

What we can do with it: A clean paired 2D reference (breath-hold vs real-time at one slice) + end-expiration indices → study real-time appearance and respiratory-correction in 2D without our own k-space recon.

Caveats: Single-slice → not a 3D SVR input/reference. Recon images only (no k-space). 2D companion, not a volumetric benchmark.

Free-running 5D references — Zenodo 15033956 (cardiopulmonary) · 13868462 (FISS) · 7621356/7615780 (fat-fraction) Tier B

AccessPublic Zenodo, CC-BY-4.0. 15033956: per-file .mat (recon outputs 78 MB–6.5 GB). 13868462: one 34 GB zip. 7621356: 4–6 GB/vol.
Formatraw k-space + shipped motion-resolved recon outputs (MATLAB .mat / .7z)
ContentFree-running, ECG-free, cardiac×respiratory-resolved whole-heart. 15033956 = N=1 demo (ships 5D recon, isotropic); 13868462 = 5 vols (25 cardiac × 4 respiratory); fat-fraction = 10 vols (GRE multi-echo).
Verified✓ 15033956 imgNufft1.mat downloaded+rendered (78 MB); 13868462 + 7621356 accessibility (HEAD).

What we can do with it: The only open 5D motion-resolved references (the slow gold-standard our pitch replaces). Use end-expiration + cardiac-phase bins as a Tier-B scoring target; or prototype self-gating against the shipped recons.

Caveats: Whole-heart 3D-radial / GRE, not bSSFP SAX cine → contrast + orientation mismatch (reslice to SAX needed). Small N (1–10). Large files.

6 · Confirmed negatives & the Tier-A gap

No public same-subject paired breath-hold + real-time SAX dataset exists. Every paired comparison in the literature (database-free DL real-time cine, GAIN, the EHJ real-time-vs-standard randomized study) uses private clinical data with no release. So a clean Tier-A answer key for real-time free-breathing SAX stacks is not obtainable off-the-shelf — the realistic options are Tier-B (reconstruct a reference from k-space) or Tier-C (self-consistency), or scanning a small paired set yourself.
  • fastMRI (NYU) — knee/brain/prostate only; no cardiac data (the CMRxRecon papers cite this as their motivation).
  • TCIA — DICOM images only, no raw k-space; no dedicated cardiac-cine collection surfaced.
  • mridata.org — JS catalog wouldn't load; known projects are knee/brain/abdominal; no confirmed cardiac cine (check the live /list).
  • LGE challenges (Emidec, MyoPS, MyoSAIQ, LAScarQS, AtriaSeg) — scar / left-atrium, not cine.
  • Fetal cardiac cine — no public downloadable dataset (methods papers only).
  • On-request / code-only — SYMPHONIC, pediatric ferumoxytol 5D, M-DIP, DRAPR, CUTE, diffusion-spiral: not directly downloadable.
  • CMRxRecon 2023/24 — k-space + SAX but breath-hold = our training domain (we train on CMRxRecon2024); Synapse+DUA. No transfer value.

7 · Recommendation & honest caveats

Recommended path. Use OCMR (Cartesian) and the Göttingen radial set as the two real real-time free-breathing SAX-stack inputs. Step 1 — run the model on both for an immediate Tier-C qualitative + self-consistency transfer read (OCMR via _archive/ocmr/ + a CS recon; Göttingen via a NUFFT recon). Step 2 — reconstruct per-scan references from their k-space for a Tier-B number, and validate the recon against the open 5D records (15033956 / 13868462). Keep Zenodo 10639392 as a 2D paired companion. For breadth, add a breath-hold generalization check (UK Biobank or CAP DETERMINE) — clearly separate from the transfer claim.
Caveats. (1) No Tier-A paired reference is publicly available for RT/FB SAX stacks. (2) Real-time data (OCMR, Göttingen) is undersampled → a reconstruction step is part of the eval (and a domain choice: Cartesian CS vs radial NUFFT). (3) 5D references are whole-heart, not SAX → require reslicing + contrast handling. (4) Field strength / vendor / trajectory differ from CMRxRecon2024 — additional intended domain shift. (5) Licenses here are non-commercial / research; some sets are DUA- or fee-gated.

Reproduce: micromamba run -n svr python tools/probe_candidate_datasets.py → micromamba run -n svr python _html/build_datasets_report.py. Manifest: result/dataset_probe/manifest.json.

8 · Download plan (nothing downloaded yet)

Status: no datasets downloaded. A trial pull was attempted but removed — local GPFS storage is constrained (writes failed despite df reporting free space), so we are holding off until the real writable budget is confirmed. The table below is the recommended selective set to fetch once space is sorted, not an on-disk inventory.

Recommended first pull (selective subsets)

DatasetSubsetRoleSize
OCMRSAX stacks only (47 real-time us_* + 11 gated fs_*); skip single-slice + LAXRT/FB SAX input (Cartesian)~72 GB
Göttingen radial bSSFPpart 1/5 (~14 subjects)RT/FB SAX input (radial)~45 GB
5D cardiopulmonaryrecon files only; skip ~24 GB raw echo k-space5D reference seed (N=1)~14 GB
Total (full recommended set)~131 GB

A much smaller start is viable: just OCMR's real-time free-breathing SAX stacks ≈ ~12 GB is enough to begin running the model on real data. Fetch script: tools/fetch_eval_datasets.py (selective, resumable) — but confirm the GPFS budget first.
